hero.title: 5 Days Bali +
hero.intro: Everything Bali is famous for — Uluwatu, Ubud, the Floating Temple, Tanah Lot — plus a full day on Nusa Penida Island visiting Kelingking Beach, Angel Billabong, Broken Beach and Crystal Bay.

breadcrumb.label: 5 Days Bali + Nusa Penida Island Tour

hero.heroImageAlt: Kelingking Beach Nusa Penida Bali cliff T-Rex viewpoint turquoise water

includes: All meals as specified in itinerary
Kecak Fire Dance at Uluwatu (optional RM35)
BBQ seafood dinner at Jimbaran Bay
excludes: ETIQA Travel Insurance Area 2 Gold RM41

overview.heading: Five Days Covering Bali and Its Most Dramatic Island Neighbour
body: Nusa Penida is Bali's most spectacular day trip — and this five-day package dedicates an entire day to it. The fast boat from Sanur Beach takes roughly 30-45 minutes to reach the island, where Kelingking Beach (the T-Rex rock viewpoint), the natural sea-pool of Angel Billabong, the arch of Broken Beach, and the clear waters of Crystal Bay each occupy a different corner of this rugged, undeveloped island. The scenery is unlike anything else in the Bali archipelago.

The remaining four days cover Bali's greatest landmarks in a logical sequence: Uluwatu's clifftop and optional Kecak Dance on arrival, the full Ubud highland circuit on day two, Bedugul's Floating Temple and Tanah Lot on day three, and the Nusa Penida fast boat on day four. Day five is a relaxed departure. For travellers who want both Bali and its most photogenic island neighbour in a single private trip, this five-day itinerary is the one.

items.title: Kelingking Beach — Nusa Penida's Iconic T-Rex Viewpoint
items.body: Kelingking is consistently ranked among the world's most dramatic viewpoints. The cliff formation — which from above resembles a Tyrannosaurus rex's head — plunges 200 metres to a crescent of white sand visible only from the clifftop. The deep turquoise water against the white sand against the green jungle is one of Southeast Asia's most otherworldly natural photographs.
items.imageAlt: Kelingking Beach Nusa Penida T-Rex cliff viewpoint turquoise Bali

items.title: Angel Billabong & Broken Beach
items.body: Angel Billabong is a natural infinity pool carved into the rock at the island's edge, filled with crystal-clear water that shifts between emerald and turquoise with the light. At low tide it is possible to descend into the pool. A short walk away is Broken Beach — a dramatic natural arch where the ocean has tunnelled through the clifftop, creating a circular cove with the sea visible through the stone roof.
items.imageAlt: Angel Billabong natural infinity pool Nusa Penida Bali

items.title: Crystal Bay — Snorkelling and Scenic Views
items.body: Crystal Bay on Nusa Penida's west coast lives up to its name — the water is extraordinarily clear, and the bay offers some of the best snorkelling in the Bali archipelago. The calm, shallow water is home to coral gardens, reef fish, and occasionally Mola-mola (ocean sunfish) during July-October. Even without snorkelling, the bay is simply beautiful.
items.imageAlt: Crystal Bay Nusa Penida Bali clear water snorkelling

items.title: Uluwatu Clifftop & Optional Kecak Dance
items.body: Uluwatu's 70-metre sea cliff is the dramatic first sight of Bali for this package. The clifftop walk around Pura Luhur Uluwatu temple offers ocean views in every direction. The optional Kecak Fire Dance performed at sunset here (RM35) is one of the most atmospheric cultural performances in Southeast Asia — over a hundred performers chanting against a backdrop of the Indian Ocean turning gold.
items.imageAlt: Uluwatu clifftop temple Bali Indian Ocean sunset

items.title: Tegalalang Rice Terrace & Ubud Highlands
items.body: The UNESCO-listed Tegalalang subak rice terraces cascade down a river valley north of Ubud in neat emerald steps — a landscape created by the interlocking traditional irrigation system that has managed Bali's water for centuries. Combined with the Ubud Royal Palace, Traditional Market, Celuk silver village, and a highland coffee plantation, day two gives a complete picture of Bali's cultural heartland.
items.imageAlt: Tegalalang rice terrace Ubud Bali subak irrigation system

items.title: Tanah Lot at Sunset & Bedugul Floating Temple
items.body: Day three pairs two of Bali's most iconic landmarks. The Pura Ulun Danu Beratan appears to float on the surface of Lake Beratan in the cool Bedugul highlands, framed by mist and mountains. The day ends at Tanah Lot, where the 16th-century sea temple on its rocky islet frames one of Bali's most memorable sunset silhouettes.
items.imageAlt: Tanah Lot sea temple Bali sunset silhouette ocean

days.title: Arrive Bali — Uluwatu, Optional Kecak Dance & Jimbaran Bay BBQ
days.description: Your driver-guide meets you at Ngurah Rai Airport and heads directly to Bali's southern peninsula. Melasti Beach is a photogenic first stop — a white-sand cove between towering limestone cliffs. Then up to Uluwatu for the clifftop walk and ocean views. The optional Kecak Fire Dance at sunset is available for RM35 — highly recommended if your arrival time permits. The evening ends with grilled BBQ seafood dinner at Jimbaran Bay on the beach sand. Optional candlelight dinner upgrade available (RM90).
suggestedActivities: Optional Kecak Fire Dance at sunset (RM35)
BBQ seafood dinner at Jimbaran Bay

days.title: Ubud Highlands, Tegalalang, Mount Batur & Tirta Empul
days.description: A full day in Bali's cultural highlands. The route moves through Celuk village's gold and silver workshops, Ubud Royal Palace (Puri Saren Agung), and the busy Ubud Traditional Market. Tegalalang's terraced rice fields offer a photo stop — an optional Bali Swing above the valley is available (RM68). A local coffee plantation follows. Lunch is at Grand Puncak Sari with a panoramic view of Mount Batur's active crater and caldera lake. The afternoon ends at Tirta Empul, a sacred spring temple founded in 962 AD. Dinner at Fork Bakery and Grill in Kuta.
suggestedActivities: Celuk Village gold and silver workshops
Tegalalang Rice Terrace (optional Bali Swing RM68)
Lunch with Mount Batur and Lake Batur views
Dinner at Fork Bakery and Grill Kuta

days.title: Bedugul Highlands, Floating Temple & Tanah Lot Sunset
days.description: The cool Bedugul highlands are the morning destination. Bloom Garden showcases highland tropical flowers before the main attraction: Pura Ulun Danu Beratan, the Floating Temple on Lake Beratan. Lunch is at Ulun Danu Resto with lake views. The afternoon visits Junglegold Chocolate Factory, where Balinese cacao is processed into artisanal chocolate, before descending to the coast for Tanah Lot at sunset. Optional one-hour Balinese full body massage available (RM90). Dinner at Tempong Indra.
suggestedActivities: Pura Ulun Danu Beratan (Floating Temple) on Lake Beratan
Lunch at Ulun Danu Resto with lake views

days.title: Nusa Penida Island — Kelingking, Angel Billabong, Broken Beach & Crystal Bay
days.description: An early start to Sanur Beach for the fast boat crossing to Nusa Penida — roughly 30-45 minutes across the Badung Strait. The island tour visits the day's four headline sites: Angel Billabong, Bali's most dramatic natural tide pool; the sea arch of Broken Beach; the jaw-dropping Kelingking Beach viewpoint (the T-Rex rock); and Crystal Bay for its extraordinary clear water and snorkelling. Return boat to Sanur in the late afternoon. Dinner at Fork Bakery & Grill Kuta.
suggestedActivities: Fast boat from Sanur Beach to Nusa Penida
Crystal Bay scenic views and snorkelling
Dinner at Fork Bakery & Grill Kuta

days.title: Leisure & Departure
days.description: A relaxed final morning — breakfast at the hotel, free time for any last-minute exploration or souvenirs, then a comfortable airport transfer to Ngurah Rai International Airport.

whyBook.heading: Why Book Your Bali + Nusa Penida Trip Through Dhesu
whyBook.body: Dhesu has been sending Malaysian travellers to Bali since 1988, and to Nusa Penida since the island became accessible to visitors. The Nusa Penida fast boat, island driver, and Kelingking trek are coordinated to maximise your time on the island without feeling rushed.

sections.heading: Bali and Nusa Penida Package from Malaysia — 5 Days 4 Nights
sections.body: Nusa Penida is a 30-minute fast boat ride from Sanur Beach on Bali's east coast, and it looks like a different world — dramatic limestone cliffs, wild surf, and beaches so picture-perfect they appear almost unreal. This 5-day package from Malaysia includes the full Nusa Penida island circuit (Kelingking, Angel Billabong, Broken Beach, Crystal Bay) alongside all of Bali's most important cultural landmarks in a single private tour. Valid for daily departure until 31 March 2027.

sections.heading: Kelingking Beach — What to Expect
sections.body: Kelingking is reached via a rough cliffside viewpoint from the parking area above — no descent to the beach is included in this package as the trail is very steep and takes 30-45 minutes each way. The view from the top is the payoff: the T-Rex rock formation, the white sand crescent 200 metres below, and the extraordinary turquoise water. Most visitors find the viewpoint fully satisfying without the descent.

sections.heading: Why Include Nusa Penida in Your Bali Trip?
sections.body: Bali receives over five million visitors per year; Nusa Penida receives a fraction of that. The island's roads are rough, the cliffs are raw, and the beaches are gloriously undeveloped — it is Bali as it was thirty years ago. Adding Nusa Penida to a Bali itinerary takes one day and adds a completely different visual dimension to the trip. For photographers and Instagram-conscious travellers, it is the single best addition available.

items.question: Is the Nusa Penida fast boat rough?
items.answer: The crossing from Sanur to Nusa Penida takes approximately 30-45 minutes. The Badung Strait can be choppy, particularly in the afternoon. Morning crossings are generally calmer. Motion sickness medication is recommended for those who are sensitive.

items.question: Can I swim at Kelingking Beach?
items.answer: Kelingking Beach's waters have strong currents and are generally not safe for swimming. The experience is the view from the clifftop, not the water. Crystal Bay is the swimming and snorkelling location on the Nusa Penida day trip.

items.question: Is there an entrance fee for Nusa Penida?
items.answer: Entrance fees for Nusa Penida's sites (Angel Billabong, Broken Beach, Kelingking, Crystal Bay) are included in the package price. The Bali levy tax of IDR 150,000 applies to international visitors to Indonesia and is listed separately in exclusions.

items.question: What shoes should I wear for Nusa Penida?
items.answer: Closed-toe sports shoes or trail shoes are strongly recommended for Nusa Penida. The clifftop paths at Kelingking and Broken Beach are uneven and can be slippery. Sandals and flat shoes are not suitable.

items.question: Can I upgrade the Nusa Penida day to include snorkelling?
items.answer: Yes. Crystal Bay is a prime snorkelling location. Contact Dhesu to add snorkelling equipment rental to your Nusa Penida day.
